rawText:
India's government, which has long cultivated an obsession with rankings and records -- from the most saplings planted in an hour to the most pledges received for an AI campaign -- now finds itself confronting an inconvenient statistical revision that shrank the economy by 3.3 per cent and widened the gap with Japan, a humbling reminder that chest-thumping about being the world's fourth-largest economy was, at least for the moment, premature.

The methodological update, which reset the base year to 2022 and incorporated new data sources that capture a more rural and less service-dependent picture of the economy, revealed that India's GDP stands at just under four trillion dollars -- smaller than previously believed, yet paradoxically growing faster, at 7.1 per cent in fiscal year 2024-25, than the earlier estimate of 6.5 per cent suggested.

While officials and a jingoistic media encourage Indians to see only America and China as peers, arithmetic remains a cruel master: India's economy is a fifth the size of China's and an eighth as big as America's, and no amount of invocations of Germany or Japan can disguise the sewage that seeps into drinking water, the bridges that collapse with metronomic regularity, or the pavements that somehow materialise in Uzbekistan but not in the world's supposed fourth-largest economy.

The gap between aggregate GDP and lived experience grows all the more glaring when measured through purchasing-power parity, which places India's per-person income closer to Jordan than to Japan -- an observation that returning holidaymakers confirm with barely believed tales of clean air, drivable roads, and rubbish collection in countries far poorer than India claims to be.

Against this backdrop of statistical sobriety, the war in the Middle East has exposed a vulnerability that no GDP ranking can remedy: India's acute dependence on imported liquefied petroleum gas, sixty per cent of which flows through the Strait of Hormuz, where the passage of even a single tanker now requires ship-by-ship negotiations with Iran's government.

The gas crunch, which has shuttered restaurants, idled fertiliser plants, and forced families in Delhi to survive on roti and bananas, is the bitter fruit of a past success -- a decade in which welfare schemes doubled the number of households using LPG but in which the government did almost nothing to diversify supply or boost domestic production.

Yet even as the crisis hammers ordinary Indians, the country is embarking on what may be its most consequential economic pivot in a generation: free-trade agreements with the European Union and the United States that could transform one of the world's most protectionist economies into a near-open one, with tariffs outside agriculture falling to unprecedentedly low levels within seven to ten years.

For a government that has been protectionist by conviction -- repudiating a quarter-century-old domestic consensus in favour of gradual liberalisation and occasionally deploying instruments that recalled the worst of the licence-quota raj -- these agreements represent a remarkable reversal, driven less by ideological conversion than by the pragmatic recognition that India's manufacturing share of low-skilled global exports, at a dismal two to three per cent, risks being left behind as capital fleeing China is diverted elsewhere.

Amid these seismic shifts, Bangalore's new breed of tech billionaires -- engineers who rode public transport and wrangled with public services before founding trading platforms and biotech firms -- are quietly pioneering institution-building philanthropy that funds not merely schools and clinics but biometric identity systems, brain-research labs, and urban-governance reforms, offering a model of private engagement that complements rather than substitutes for the state.

India's reality check, then, is not a story of failure but of cognitive dissonance -- a nation that simultaneously prints world-beating growth figures and cannot supply cooking gas to its citizens, that negotiates landmark trade deals while its statistics ministry concedes the economy is smaller than anyone thought, and whose trajectory will depend less on which ordinal rank it claims than on whether it can convert ambition into the basic safety and services that would constitute a record truly worth celebrating.

sentences.original: India's government, which has long cultivated an obsession with rankings and records -- from the most saplings planted in an hour to the most pledges received for an AI campaign -- now finds itself confronting an inconvenient statistical revision that shrank the economy by 3.3 per cent and widened the gap with Japan, a humbling reminder that chest-thumping about being the world's fourth-largest economy was, at least for the moment, premature.
sentences.translation: 순위와 기록에 대한 집착을 오랫동안 키워온 인도 정부는 -- 한 시간에 가장 많은 묘목을 심은 기록부터 AI 캠페인 서약 최다 수령까지 -- 이제 경제 규모를 3.3퍼센트 줄이고 일본과의 격차를 벌려 놓은 불편한 통계 수정과 마주하고 있으며, 이는 세계 4위 경제 대국이라는 가슴 부풀리기가 적어도 당분간은 시기상조였음을 일깨워 주는 겸허한 현실이다.

grammarElements.text: India's government, which has long cultivated an obsession with rankings and records
grammarElements.label: 관계절이 삽입된 주어

grammarElements.text: now finds itself confronting
grammarElements.label: find + 재귀대명사 + 현재분사

grammarElements.text: an inconvenient statistical revision
grammarElements.label: 목적어

grammarElements.text: that shrank the economy by 3.3 per cent
grammarElements.label: 관계절 (수정의 결과)

grammarElements.text: a humbling reminder that ...
grammarElements.label: 동격 보충 설명

vocabulary.example: It would be premature to declare victory before the data are confirmed.
The firm cultivated a reputation for reliability over many years.

expressions.usage: 국가나 기업이 과장된 성과를 과시할 때 비판적으로 사용하는 표현

patterns.explanation: 주체가 예상치 못한 상황에 처했음을 강조하는 패턴
examples: The company now finds itself competing against its own former partners.
The government now finds itself defending policies it once opposed.

drillQuestions.question: India's government now finds itself ______ an inconvenient statistical revision.

sentences.notes: find oneself ~ing 구문으로, 인도 정부가 원치 않게 불편한 현실과 맞닥뜨린 상황을 부각한다.

koreanAlignment.en: India's government, which has long cultivated an obsession with rankings and records
koreanAlignment.ko: 순위와 기록에 대한 집착을 오랫동안 키워온 인도 정부

koreanAlignment.en: an inconvenient statistical revision
shrank the economy by 3.3 per cent
chest-thumping about being the world's fourth-largest economy

pronunciationNotes: confronting에서 /ɒn/은 한국어 '온'보다 입을 더 크게 벌린다

sentences.original: The methodological update, which reset the base year to 2022 and incorporated new data sources that capture a more rural and less service-dependent picture of the economy, revealed that India's GDP stands at just under four trillion dollars -- smaller than previously believed, yet paradoxically growing faster, at 7.1 per cent in fiscal year 2024-25, than the earlier estimate of 6.5 per cent suggested.
sentences.translation: 기준연도를 2022년으로 재설정하고, 경제의 보다 농촌 중심적이고 서비스업 의존도가 낮은 면모를 포착하는 새로운 데이터 소스를 반영한 이번 방법론 업데이트는, 인도의 GDP가 4조 달러를 약간 밑돈다는 사실을 드러냈다 -- 이전 추정보다 규모는 작지만, 역설적으로 2024-25 회계연도 성장률은 기존 추정치 6.5퍼센트보다 빠른 7.1퍼센트였다.

grammarElements.text: The methodological update, which reset ... and incorporated ...
grammarElements.label: 관계절이 삽입된 주어

grammarElements.text: revealed
grammarElements.label: 본동사

grammarElements.text: that India's GDP stands at just under four trillion dollars
grammarElements.label: 명사절 (that절 목적어)

grammarElements.text: smaller than previously believed, yet paradoxically growing faster
grammarElements.label: 대조적 병렬 수식어

vocabulary.example: The policy paradoxically made the problem it aimed to solve even worse.

expressions.usage: 수치를 정밀하게 설명하되 '약간 못 미침'을 표현할 때 사용

patterns.explanation: 핵심 사실을 밝힌 뒤 대시로 대조적인 두 측면을 동시에 제시하는 패턴
examples: The audit revealed that profits had fallen -- less than feared, yet still below the target.
The survey revealed that turnout rose -- lower than in 2020, yet higher than analysts predicted.

drillQuestions.question: The methodological update revealed that India's GDP stands at just ______ four trillion dollars.

sentences.notes: yet paradoxically라는 전환으로 '규모 축소'와 '성장률 상승'이라는 모순된 사실을 한 문장에 녹여냈다.

koreanAlignment.en: The methodological update
reset the base year to 2022
just under four trillion dollars
paradoxically growing faster

sentences.original: While officials and a jingoistic media encourage Indians to see only America and China as peers, arithmetic remains a cruel master: India's economy is a fifth the size of China's and an eighth as big as America's, and no amount of invocations of Germany or Japan can disguise the sewage that seeps into drinking water, the bridges that collapse with metronomic regularity, or the pavements that somehow materialise in Uzbekistan but not in the world's supposed fourth-largest economy.
sentences.translation: 관료들과 국수주의적 언론이 인도인들에게 미국과 중국만을 동류로 보라고 부추기는 동안, 산술은 냉혹한 주인으로 남아 있다: 인도 경제는 중국의 5분의 1이고 미국의 8분의 1에 불과하며, 독일이나 일본을 아무리 거론해도 식수에 스며드는 하수, 메트로놈처럼 규칙적으로 무너지는 다리, 우즈베키스탄에는 있지만 세계 4위 경제 대국이라는 나라에는 없는 보도를 감출 수 없다.

grammarElements.text: While officials and a jingoistic media encourage Indians to see ...
grammarElements.label: 양보절 (While)

grammarElements.text: arithmetic remains a cruel master
grammarElements.label: 주절 주어 + 동사 + 보어

grammarElements.text: no amount of invocations ... can disguise
grammarElements.label: no amount of + 동사

grammarElements.text: the sewage ..., the bridges ..., or the pavements ...
grammarElements.label: 병렬 목적어 (삼중 나열)

vocabulary.example: The editorial took a jingoistic tone that ignored the country's real challenges.
The factory reported breakdowns with metronomic regularity.

expressions.usage: 어떤 노력도 결과를 바꿀 수 없음을 강하게 부정할 때 쓰는 표현

patterns.pattern: While [group] encourage [audience] to [believe X], [reality] remains [harsh truth]
patterns.explanation: 공식 담론과 냉혹한 현실 사이의 괴리를 대비시키는 패턴
examples: While leaders encourage citizens to stay calm, inflation remains a daily burden.
While brochures encourage tourists to visit, crime remains a serious concern.

drillQuestions.question: Arithmetic remains a cruel ______: India's economy is a fifth the size of China's.

sentences.notes: arithmetic remains a cruel master라는 의인화로 통계적 현실을 냉정하게 대비시키며, 삼중 나열(하수, 다리, 보도)이 수사적 효과를 극대화한다.

koreanAlignment.en: a jingoistic media
arithmetic remains a cruel master
no amount of invocations
with metronomic regularity

sentences.original: The gap between aggregate GDP and lived experience grows all the more glaring when measured through purchasing-power parity, which places India's per-person income closer to Jordan than to Japan -- an observation that returning holidaymakers confirm with barely believed tales of clean air, drivable roads, and rubbish collection in countries far poorer than India claims to be.
sentences.translation: 총 GDP와 체감 현실 사이의 격차는 구매력 평가로 측정하면 한층 더 극명해지는데, 이 기준으로 인도의 1인당 소득은 일본보다 요르단에 더 가깝다 -- 휴가에서 돌아온 사람들이 인도보다 훨씬 가난하다는 나라의 깨끗한 공기, 운전할 만한 도로, 쓰레기 수거에 대해 믿기 어려운 이야기를 전하며 확인해 주는 현실이다.

grammarElements.text: The gap between aggregate GDP and lived experience
grammarElements.label: 주어

grammarElements.text: grows all the more glaring
grammarElements.label: 동사 + 비교급 강조

grammarElements.text: when measured through purchasing-power parity
grammarElements.label: 조건/시간 부사절

grammarElements.text: which places India's per-person income closer to Jordan than to Japan
grammarElements.label: 비제한적 관계절

vocabulary.example: Aggregate demand may look strong, but individual spending is uneven.

expressions.usage: 이미 뚜렷한 문제가 특정 조건에서 더욱 부각될 때 사용

patterns.pattern: The gap between [A] and [B] grows all the more [adjective] when [condition]
patterns.explanation: 두 가지 사이의 괴리가 특정 조건 아래에서 더 심해짐을 보여주는 패턴
examples: The gap between policy and practice grows all the more obvious when budgets are cut.
The gap between rhetoric and reality grows all the more painful when voters compare promises with outcomes.

drillQuestions.question: The gap between aggregate GDP and lived experience grows all the more ______ when measured through purchasing-power parity.

sentences.notes: closer to Jordan than to Japan이라는 비교가 통계적 현실의 충격을 극대화하며, 휴가객의 일화를 증거로 활용하는 수법이 인상적이다.

koreanAlignment.en: The gap between aggregate GDP and lived experience
koreanAlignment.ko: 총 GDP와 체감 현실 사이의 격차

koreanAlignment.en: purchasing-power parity
closer to Jordan than to Japan
returning holidaymakers

pronunciationNotes: aggregate: /ˈæɡ.rɪ.ɡət/ 첫 음절에 강세, 마지막은 약화된 /ət/

sentences.original: Against this backdrop of statistical sobriety, the war in the Middle East has exposed a vulnerability that no GDP ranking can remedy: India's acute dependence on imported liquefied petroleum gas, sixty per cent of which flows through the Strait of Hormuz, where the passage of even a single tanker now requires ship-by-ship negotiations with Iran's government.
sentences.translation: 이러한 통계적 냉정함을 배경으로, 중동 전쟁은 어떤 GDP 순위로도 해결할 수 없는 취약점을 드러냈다: 인도가 수입 액화석유가스에 심각하게 의존하고 있다는 점이며, 그 60퍼센트는 호르무즈 해협을 통과하는데, 이제 유조선 한 척이 지나가는 것조차 이란 정부와 건별 협상을 필요로 한다.

grammarElements.text: Against this backdrop of statistical sobriety
grammarElements.label: 전치사구 부사어

grammarElements.text: the war in the Middle East
grammarElements.label: 주어

grammarElements.text: has exposed
grammarElements.label: 현재완료 동사

grammarElements.text: a vulnerability that no GDP ranking can remedy
grammarElements.label: 관계절이 붙은 목적어

grammarElements.text: sixty per cent of which flows through the Strait of Hormuz
grammarElements.label: 전치사 + 관계대명사 계속적 용법

vocabulary.example: No single policy can remedy decades of underinvestment.

expressions.usage: 앞서 설명한 맥락 위에 새로운 사건을 소개할 때 사용하는 전환 표현

patterns.pattern: Against this backdrop of [context], [event] has exposed [vulnerability]
patterns.explanation: 기존 맥락 위에 새로운 위기나 발견을 도입하는 전환 패턴
examples: Against this backdrop of fiscal austerity, the earthquake exposed the fragility of the nation's infrastructure.
Against this backdrop of rising inequality, the pandemic exposed the gaps in the health system.

drillQuestions.question: Against this backdrop of statistical sobriety, the war has exposed a vulnerability that no GDP ranking can ______.

sentences.notes: 콜론(:) 뒤에 구체적 취약점을 제시하고, 관계절을 중첩시켜 의존의 경로(LPG → 호르무즈 해협 → 이란)를 단계적으로 드러낸다.

koreanAlignment.en: Against this backdrop of statistical sobriety
a vulnerability that no GDP ranking can remedy
koreanAlignment.ko: 어떤 GDP 순위로도 해결할 수 없는 취약점

koreanAlignment.en: acute dependence on imported liquefied petroleum gas
ship-by-ship negotiations

sentences.original: The gas crunch, which has shuttered restaurants, idled fertiliser plants, and forced families in Delhi to survive on roti and bananas, is the bitter fruit of a past success -- a decade in which welfare schemes doubled the number of households using LPG but in which the government did almost nothing to diversify supply or boost domestic production.
sentences.translation: 식당을 문 닫게 하고, 비료 공장을 멈추게 하고, 델리의 가정들을 로티와 바나나로 연명하게 만든 가스 대란은 과거 성공의 쓴 열매다 -- 복지 제도가 LPG 사용 가구 수를 두 배로 늘린 10년이었지만, 정부가 공급 다변화나 국내 생산 확대에는 거의 아무것도 하지 않은 10년이기도 했다.

grammarElements.text: The gas crunch, which has shuttered ..., idled ..., and forced ...
grammarElements.label: 관계절이 삽입된 주어 (삼중 병렬)

grammarElements.text: is
grammarElements.label: be동사

grammarElements.text: the bitter fruit of a past success
grammarElements.label: 보어 (은유적)

grammarElements.text: a decade in which ... but in which ...
grammarElements.label: 대조적 관계절 (동격)

vocabulary.example: Rising costs have shuttered dozens of small businesses across the city.

expressions.usage: 과거의 결정이 현재 부정적 결과로 돌아왔을 때 사용하는 은유적 표현

patterns.pattern: [Crisis] is the bitter fruit of [past success] -- [period] in which [achievement] but in which [failure]
patterns.explanation: 현재 위기가 과거 성공의 역설적 산물임을 밝히고, 성공과 실패를 대비시키는 패턴
examples: The housing crash was the bitter fruit of a credit boom -- a decade in which lending surged but in which regulators looked the other way.
The skills shortage is the bitter fruit of earlier automation -- a period in which output rose but in which training budgets were slashed.

drillQuestions.question: The gas crunch is the bitter ______ of a past success.

sentences.notes: bitter fruit of a past success라는 은유가 '성공이 곧 재앙의 원인'이라는 역설을 함축적으로 전달한다.

koreanAlignment.en: The gas crunch
shuttered restaurants, idled fertiliser plants, and forced families
koreanAlignment.ko: 식당을 문 닫게 하고, 비료 공장을 멈추게 하고, 가정들을 연명하게 만든

koreanAlignment.en: the bitter fruit of a past success
diversify supply or boost domestic production

sentences.original: Yet even as the crisis hammers ordinary Indians, the country is embarking on what may be its most consequential economic pivot in a generation: free-trade agreements with the European Union and the United States that could transform one of the world's most protectionist economies into a near-open one, with tariffs outside agriculture falling to unprecedentedly low levels within seven to ten years.
sentences.translation: 그러나 위기가 평범한 인도인들을 강타하는 와중에도, 이 나라는 한 세대 만에 가장 중대한 경제적 전환에 착수하고 있다: EU 및 미국과의 자유무역협정은 세계에서 가장 보호주의적인 경제 중 하나를 거의 개방 경제로 탈바꿈시킬 수 있으며, 농업 외 관세는 7~10년 안에 전례 없이 낮은 수준으로 떨어질 전망이다.

grammarElements.text: Yet even as the crisis hammers ordinary Indians
grammarElements.label: 양보의 부사절 (even as)

grammarElements.text: the country is embarking on
grammarElements.label: 진행형 동사구

grammarElements.text: what may be its most consequential economic pivot in a generation
grammarElements.label: 명사절 (what절)

grammarElements.text: free-trade agreements ... that could transform ...
grammarElements.label: 콜론 뒤 동격 + 관계절

vocabulary.example: This may be the most consequential policy shift of the decade.
The country's protectionist stance has isolated it from global supply chains.

expressions.usage: 중요하고 규모가 큰 새로운 일이나 변화를 시작할 때 사용

patterns.pattern: Yet even as [crisis], [subject] is embarking on [transformative action]
patterns.explanation: 위기와 동시에 진행되는 대전환을 대비시키는 패턴
examples: Yet even as layoffs mount, the firm is embarking on its largest acquisition.
Yet even as the currency weakens, the central bank is embarking on a rate-cutting cycle.

drillQuestions.question: The country is embarking on what may be its most ______ economic pivot in a generation.

sentences.notes: even as 구문이 '위기 한복판에서도 대전환이 진행 중'이라는 극적인 동시성을 만든다.

koreanAlignment.en: Yet even as the crisis hammers ordinary Indians
its most consequential economic pivot in a generation
koreanAlignment.ko: 한 세대 만에 가장 중대한 경제적 전환

koreanAlignment.en: one of the world's most protectionist economies
koreanAlignment.ko: 세계에서 가장 보호주의적인 경제 중 하나

koreanAlignment.en: unprecedentedly low levels

sentences.original: For a government that has been protectionist by conviction -- repudiating a quarter-century-old domestic consensus in favour of gradual liberalisation and occasionally deploying instruments that recalled the worst of the licence-quota raj -- these agreements represent a remarkable reversal, driven less by ideological conversion than by the pragmatic recognition that India's manufacturing share of low-skilled global exports, at a dismal two to three per cent, risks being left behind as capital fleeing China is diverted elsewhere.
sentences.translation: 확신에 의한 보호주의를 고수해 온 정부로서는 -- 점진적 자유화를 지지하던 25년 된 국내 합의를 부정하고, 때로는 허가-할당 시대의 최악을 연상시키는 수단을 동원하면서 -- 이번 협정들은 놀라운 반전이며, 이념적 전향보다는 인도의 저숙련 글로벌 수출 제조업 비중이 참담한 2~3퍼센트에 불과해 중국에서 빠져나가는 자본이 다른 곳으로 향할 위험이 있다는 실용적 인식에 의해 추동된 것이다.

grammarElements.text: For a government that has been protectionist by conviction
grammarElements.label: 전치사구 (배경 설정)

grammarElements.text: these agreements represent a remarkable reversal
grammarElements.label: 주절 주어 + 동사 + 목적어

grammarElements.text: driven less by ... than by ...
grammarElements.label: 과거분사 수식어 (비교 구문)

grammarElements.text: that India's manufacturing share ... risks being left behind
grammarElements.label: 동격 that절

vocabulary.example: The new administration repudiated the trade agreements signed by its predecessor.

expressions.phrase: driven less by A than by B
expressions.usage: 표면적 동기보다 진짜 동기를 밝히는 분석적 표현

patterns.pattern: [Outcome] is driven less by [apparent cause] than by [real cause]
patterns.explanation: 겉보기 원인이 아니라 실질적 원인을 밝히는 분석 패턴
examples: The merger was driven less by synergy than by the CEO's personal ambition.
The reform was driven less by ideology than by fiscal desperation.

drillQuestions.question: These agreements represent a remarkable ______, driven less by ideological conversion than by pragmatic recognition.

sentences.notes: driven less by ... than by 구문으로 정책 전환의 진짜 동기(실용적 위기감)를 드러내며, licence-quota raj라는 역사적 레퍼런스가 보호주의의 깊이를 환기한다.

koreanAlignment.en: protectionist by conviction
a remarkable reversal
driven less by ideological conversion than by pragmatic recognition
koreanAlignment.ko: 이념적 전향보다는 실용적 인식에 의해 추동된

koreanAlignment.en: at a dismal two to three per cent

sentences.original: Amid these seismic shifts, Bangalore's new breed of tech billionaires -- engineers who rode public transport and wrangled with public services before founding trading platforms and biotech firms -- are quietly pioneering institution-building philanthropy that funds not merely schools and clinics but biometric identity systems, brain-research labs, and urban-governance reforms, offering a model of private engagement that complements rather than substitutes for the state.
sentences.translation: 이러한 지각변동 속에서, 방갈로르의 신흥 기술 억만장자들 -- 트레이딩 플랫폼과 바이오테크 기업을 설립하기 전에 대중교통을 타고 공공서비스와 씨름했던 엔지니어들 -- 은 단순히 학교나 병원이 아닌 생체인식 시스템, 뇌과학 연구소, 도시 거버넌스 개혁에 자금을 대는 제도 구축형 자선활동을 조용히 선도하며, 국가를 대체하는 것이 아니라 보완하는 민간 참여 모델을 제시하고 있다.

grammarElements.text: Amid these seismic shifts
grammarElements.label: 전치사구 부사어

grammarElements.text: Bangalore's new breed of tech billionaires
grammarElements.label: 주어

grammarElements.text: are quietly pioneering
grammarElements.label: 진행형 동사

grammarElements.text: institution-building philanthropy that funds not merely ... but ...
grammarElements.label: not merely ... but 병렬 목적어

grammarElements.text: offering a model ... that complements rather than substitutes for the state
grammarElements.label: 분사구문 (결과)

vocabulary.example: Their philanthropy goes beyond writing cheques to building lasting institutions.

expressions.usage: 민간과 공공의 역할 관계를 설명할 때 쓰는 정교한 표현

patterns.pattern: [Actors] are pioneering [innovation] that [does] not merely [A] but [B], offering a model that [principle]
patterns.explanation: 새로운 주체가 기존 관행을 넘어서는 혁신을 도입하고 그 원칙을 제시하는 패턴
examples: Local councils are pioneering budgets that fund not merely roads but digital infrastructure, offering a model that bridges rural and urban needs.
Universities are pioneering courses that teach not merely theory but applied skills, offering a model that employers actually value.

drillQuestions.question: They are pioneering institution-building philanthropy that funds not merely schools and clinics but biometric identity systems, brain-research labs, and urban-governance ______.

sentences.notes: not merely ... but 구문이 전통적 자선과 제도 구축형 자선의 차이를 명확히 하며, complements rather than substitutes가 핵심 원칙을 짚는다.

koreanAlignment.en: Amid these seismic shifts
Bangalore's new breed of tech billionaires
institution-building philanthropy
complements rather than substitutes for the state

pronunciationNotes: philanthropy: /fɪˈlæn.θrə.pi/ 두 번째 음절에 강세, th는 /θ/

sentences.original: India's reality check, then, is not a story of failure but of cognitive dissonance -- a nation that simultaneously prints world-beating growth figures and cannot supply cooking gas to its citizens, that negotiates landmark trade deals while its statistics ministry concedes the economy is smaller than anyone thought, and whose trajectory will depend less on which ordinal rank it claims than on whether it can convert ambition into the basic safety and services that would constitute a record truly worth celebrating.
sentences.translation: 그러므로 인도의 현실 점검은 실패의 이야기가 아니라 인지 부조화의 이야기다 -- 세계 최고 수준의 성장 수치를 찍어내면서도 국민에게 조리용 가스를 공급하지 못하는 나라, 획기적인 무역 협상을 진행하면서 통계청은 경제 규모가 누구도 생각한 것보다 작다고 시인하는 나라, 그리고 어떤 순위를 주장하느냐보다 야망을 기본적인 안전과 서비스로 전환할 수 있느냐에 궤적이 달려 있는 나라의 이야기다.

grammarElements.text: India's reality check
grammarElements.label: 주어

grammarElements.text: is not a story of failure but of cognitive dissonance
grammarElements.label: not A but B 구문

grammarElements.text: a nation that simultaneously prints ... and cannot supply ...
grammarElements.label: 동격 + 대조적 관계절

grammarElements.text: whose trajectory will depend less on ... than on ...
grammarElements.label: less ... than 비교 구문

vocabulary.example: The voter experienced cognitive dissonance between supporting lower taxes and wanting better public services.
The minister conceded that the initial projections had been too optimistic.

expressions.usage: 형식적 기록이 아닌 실질적 성과를 강조할 때 사용하는 마무리 표현

patterns.pattern: [Subject] is not a story of [A] but of [B] -- a [entity] that [contrast 1], that [contrast 2], and whose [future condition]
patterns.explanation: 결론부에서 주제를 재정의하고 대조적 요소를 삼중으로 나열하며 미래 과제를 제시하는 패턴
examples: The crisis is not a story of incompetence but of misaligned priorities -- a system that rewards speed, that punishes caution, and whose reform will require honest self-examination.
The election is not a story of betrayal but of exhaustion -- a public that demands change, that distrusts all options, and whose verdict will shape the decade ahead.

drillQuestions.question: India's reality check is not a story of failure but of cognitive ______.

sentences.notes: 마지막 문장이 글 전체를 '인지 부조화'라는 하나의 프레임으로 통합하며, 삼중 that절이 모순의 층위를 쌓아올린다.

koreanAlignment.en: not a story of failure but of cognitive dissonance
koreanAlignment.ko: 실패의 이야기가 아니라 인지 부조화의 이야기

koreanAlignment.en: simultaneously prints world-beating growth figures
koreanAlignment.ko: 세계 최고 수준의 성장 수치를 찍어내면서도

koreanAlignment.en: the statistics ministry concedes the economy is smaller than anyone thought
koreanAlignment.ko: 통계청은 경제 규모가 누구도 생각한 것보다 작다고 시인하는

koreanAlignment.en: convert ambition into the basic safety and services
